name: Tamoanchan
description: Tamoanchan is a mythical paradise and origin place in Aztec mythology. It is often described as a lush, fertile garden where the gods created humanity from ground bones and maize after the previous world had been destroyed by flood. This sacred space is not just a place of creation but also a realm of divine abundance and knowledge. It is sometimes depicted as a place of trials and tribulations as well, as the gods themselves experienced difficulties even in this paradise. Tamoanchan is considered the origin of sacred knowledge and the place where the calendar was invented. The name itself translates roughly to 'We go to seek our home,' emphasizing its role as the ultimate destination of the soul. It is a complex concept, encompassing both the idyllic past and the potential for future renewal, embodying the cyclical nature of Aztec cosmology. Tamoanchan also represents a place of refuge, a sanctuary from the chaos of the world. The concept of Tamoanchan is often associated with the west, the direction of the setting sun and the underworld, suggesting a connection between life, death, and rebirth. It is also a source of medicinal plants, further highlighting its life-giving properties.
subtitle: Mythical paradise and origin of humanity

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Humanity
keyMyths.description: After the flood, the gods gathered in Tamoanchan. They ground up the bones of previous humans and mixed them with maize, creating the new race of humans.

keyMyths.title: The Theft of Maize
keyMyths.description: The gods struggled to obtain maize for humanity in Tamoanchan. Quetzalcoatl eventually transformed into an ant to steal the maize from the mountain of sustenance.

sources.author: Miguel León-Portilla
sources.work: Aztec Thought and Culture: A Study of the Ancient Nahuatl Mind
sources.description: Discusses the philosophical and cosmological significance of Tamoanchan.
sources.text: Discusses the philosophical and cosmological significance of Tamoanchan.

sources.author: David Carrasco
sources.description: Provides an overview of the religious beliefs and practices of Mesoamerican cultures, including the Aztec concept of Tamoanchan.
